title: Unidentified New Republic Gungan diplomat

A [[Gender/Legends|male]] '''Gungan diplomat''' held an official position in the government of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]], an elderly [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] belonging to the [[Otolla/Legends|Otolla]] [[Species/Legends|subspecies]], was known for his wildlife acumen. He enjoyed bringing pets from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Republic.

==Biography==
At some point after the [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|foundation]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="SWFG" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] was appointed to an [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadorial]] function within the young government. That individual belonged to the lanky [[Otolla/Legends|Otolla]] [[Species/Legends|subspecies]] of Gungan,<ref name="SWFG" /> which were differentiated from the [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] by their elongated heads and retractable [[Eye/Legends|eyestalks]].<ref name="TPMIG">[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']]</ref>

In the course of his professional activities, he often traveled to the gleaming [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Capital/Legends|seat]] of the New Republic, with his two pets—a slavering [[Blarth/Legends|blarth]] he kept on a leash and an [[igitz]] that he transported in a [[vivarium]] shaped like a typical Gungan [[Hydrostatic bubble/Legends|hydrostatic bubble]]. At some point during his tenure, the Gungan [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] participated in some sort of cortege in which [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] ambassadors marched with their favorite pets. The illustrious [[Zoologist/Legends|zoologist]] [[Lyrre T'enna]] immortalized that event in the published edition of [[Lyrre T'enna's annotated field guide|her field guide]].<ref name="SWFG" />

==Personality and traits==
When he served the New Republic, the [[Color/Legends|orange]]-skinned diplomat was already aged, as he sported prominent flesh whiskers<ref name="SWFG" /> that grew on older Gungans.<ref name="TPMV">''[[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The diplomat enjoyed the company of domestic animals, including that of the blarths, a species of rotund and amiable creatures that had been kept as household pets by Gungans for many a [[Standard century/Legends|century]]. His care for wildlife was also considered a sign of civic pride in the [[Politics/Legends|political]] class of Coruscant.<ref name="SWFG" />

==Equipment==
He wore the long [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] patterned with [[Otoh swirls/Legends|Otoh swirls]] and the [[Leather/Legends|leather]] hood of the [[Rep/Legends|Reps]], members of the [[Gungan High Council/Legends|Gungan High Council]] of [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], capital [[City/Legends|city]] of the Gungans on Naboo.<ref name="SWFG" /><ref name="TPMV" />

==Behind the scenes==
The unidentified Gungan diplomat was featured in ''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]'', a reference book published in [[2001]] and co-authored by [[Terryl Whitlatch]] and [[Bob Carrau]]. Whitlatch also illustrated the guide,<ref name="SWFG" /> which reused many conceptual drawings she had originally created for<ref name="TWCC">{{SWArchive|url=episode-i/bts/profile/f19990210/index.html|text=Terryl Whitlatch: Creature Conjurer}}</ref> the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film<ref name="EP1">{{Film|I}}</ref> [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="TWCC" />
